comment: CIFAR-100 analogical structured edit generation for peak-search transfer checks (safe)
prompt: Task: Improve the baseline CIFAR-100 model using one constrained structured edit.
Use this source edit as a transfer hint, not as a literal template.
For this policy, prefer safe_edit unless suggested_edit is equally safe and clearly better matched to target_summary.
Emit one valid target edit JSON for the target baseline.
Transfer the improvement pattern, not every field literally.
Do not copy raw Python code.
Generate exactly 3 XML tags and nothing else:
- Prefer width="wide" when the source edit suggests a stronger architecture
- Prefer pool="adaptive_avg" over pool="keep" for safe head transfer
- Keep classifier_dropout in the safe band 0.05 to 0.2 unless the source edit clearly requires otherwise
- Avoid residual_kernel unless the source edit clearly depends on it and the target already looks compatible
- If the source edit is weak or ambiguous, emit the nearest safe target edit instead of copying it literally
- Output exactly one JSON object inside <edit>
- Use only the allowed keys above
- Start the <edit> body with JSON keys immediately, not prose
- Do not mention or explain the source edit
- If no safe architecture change is clear, set mode to "hp_transform_only"

comment: CIFAR-100 analogical structured edit generation for peak-search transfer checks (risky)
prompt: Task: Improve the baseline CIFAR-100 model using one constrained structured edit.
Use this source edit as a transfer hint, not as a literal template.
For this policy, prefer suggested_edit when it is still a valid 32x32 structured change.
Emit one higher-upside target edit JSON for the target baseline.
Transfer the improvement pattern, not every field literally.
Favor a plausible structured_cifar_edit over hp_transform_only when a real architecture change exists.
Do not copy raw Python code.
Generate exactly 3 XML tags and nothing else:
- Prefer mode="structured_cifar_edit" when the source edit suggests a plausible structural gain
- Prefer width="wide" over width="same" when the source edit points to a stronger trunk and the target is not already wide
- Prefer pool="adaptive_avg" when the source gain looks head-related and the target does not already have it
- Use residual_kernel only when the source edit clearly depends on it and the target already appears compatible
- Keep classifier_dropout in the range 0.05 to 0.2 even for risky transfer
- Use mode="hp_transform_only" only as a last resort when no plausible structured change survives the dataset constraints
- Output exactly one JSON object inside <edit>
- Use only the allowed keys above
- Start the <edit> body with JSON keys immediately, not prose
- Do not mention or explain the source edit
